title: React JSX
tldr: JSX is a syntax extension for JavaScript that looks like HTML but compiles to React.createElement calls.
JSX expressions use curly braces {} to embed JavaScript values.
Every JSX element must be closed, and adjacent elements must be wrapped in a fragment or parent element.
JSX prevents injection attacks by default - values are escaped before rendering.
laymanDefinition: JSX is like a recipe card that mixes text instructions (HTML-like tags) with variables in curly braces. {sugar} is a placeholder filled with an actual number.

deepDive.heading: JSX is Syntactic Sugar
deepDive.text: JSX compiles to React.createElement(type, props, ...children) calls at build time via Babel or TypeScript.

deepDive.heading: Embedding Expressions
deepDive.text: Any JS expression works in {}: variables, function calls, ternary, map(). Statements (if/else, for) are NOT allowed.

deepDive.heading: JSX vs HTML Differences
deepDive.text: className vs class, htmlFor vs for, camelCase properties (onClick), self-closing tags need slash, style takes object.

deepDive.heading: Fragments
deepDive.text: JSX needs a single root. Use <></> to group without adding DOM nodes.

interviewAnswer: JSX is a syntax extension for JavaScript allowing HTML-like markup in JS files. It compiles to React.createElement calls, producing VDOM objects. Values in {} are escaped, preventing XSS. camelCase attributes, style objects, and fragments are key differences from HTML.

interviewQuestions.question: What is JSX and why use it?
interviewQuestions.answer: HTML-like syntax in JS. Familiar, compile-time errors, compiles to createElement calls, allows embedding JS expressions.

interviewQuestions.question: How does JSX prevent XSS?
interviewQuestions.answer: Escapes all values in curly braces before rendering. Strings are HTML-escaped.

interviewQuestions.question: Key JSX vs HTML differences?
interviewQuestions.answer: className, htmlFor, camelCase events, style as object, closed tags, {/* comments */}.

interviewQuestions.question: Why can't JSX return two adjacent elements?
interviewQuestions.answer: createElement returns one root. Use fragments to group.

interviewQuestions.question: Can you use statements in JSX?
interviewQuestions.answer: No. Only expressions (ternary, &&, map). if/else/for are statements.

interviewQuestions.question: What are fragments?
interviewQuestions.answer: <></> groups children without adding DOM nodes.

interviewQuestions.question: How to add inline styles?
interviewQuestions.answer: As JS object with camelCase: <div style={{ backgroundColor: 'blue' }}>.

codeExamples.title: Embedding Expressions
codeExamples.description: Curly braces embed any JS expression. Ternary and && work for conditionals.

codeExamples.title: Fragments
codeExamples.description: Fragments (<></>) group children without adding DOM nodes.
